response_text:
Use tangent periodicity and angle identities.

\[
\tan 192^\circ=\tan(180^\circ+12^\circ)=\tan 12^\circ
\]
\[
\tan 48^\circ=\tan 48^\circ
\]
\[
\tan 168^\circ=\tan(180^\circ-12^\circ)=-\tan 12^\circ
\]
\[
\tan 408^\circ=\tan(360^\circ+48^\circ)=\tan 48^\circ
\]

So the expression becomes
\[
\frac{\tan 12^\circ+\tan 48^\circ}{1-\tan 12^\circ\tan 48^\circ}.
\]

Now use
\[
\tan(A+B)=\frac{\tan A+\tan B}{1-\tan A\tan B}.
\]
With \(A=12^\circ\), \(B=48^\circ\), this is
\[
\tan(12^\circ+48^\circ)=\tan 60^\circ=\sqrt3.
\]

Final answer: \(\sqrt3\)
